methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $98M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Helical Fusion is a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Takaya Taguchi and Junichi Miyazawa that has raised $98M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a broader nuclear energy sector comp universe of 11 total peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Aalo Atomics.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear energy segment—only one adjacent-stage peer and zero same-stage peers met filtering criteria, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum for a credible median. Consequently, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $98M total raised as a valuation proxy. This method yields an implied valuation of $186M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $158M (low) to $214M (high), with a median point of $186M. The range spans ±15% around the median to reflect execution risk and capital efficiency assumptions embedded in the stage-progression step-up. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.9x multiplier to this specific subsector given limited precedent, and the absence of nuclear-specific peer multiples to validate cost-of-capital assumptions. A fuller peer-based triangulation would require identifying additional same-stage nuclear development or commercialization-phase companies.
